options: Implantation in the lower uterine segment
Implantation and development of the ovum outside the normal uterine cavity
definition.explain: Ectopic pregnancy is the implantation and development of the ovum outside the normal uterine cavity.

definition.q: The most common site of ectopic pregnancy is:
definition.explain: The ampulla is the most common site for ectopic pregnancy.

risk.q: Which of the following is a risk factor for ectopic pregnancy?
risk.explain: Risk factors include previous ectopic pregnancy, tubal damage (infection/surgery), and IUD use. Wait — IUD use IS a risk factor. Let me rephrase.

risk.q: All of the following are risk factors for ectopic pregnancy EXCEPT:
risk.explain: Risk factors include previous ectopic pregnancy, PID, tubal surgery, IVF, and IUD use. Grand multiparity is not a risk factor.

tubal-sites.q: The most common site for tubal ectopic pregnancy is the:
tubal-sites.explain: Ampulla is the most common site of ectopic pregnancy.

tubal-sites.q: An interstitial (cornual) ectopic pregnancy is dangerous because:
options: It is the most common type
It ruptures late with severe hemorrhage due to rich blood supply
tubal-sites.explain: Interstitial ectopic is rare but dangerous because it ruptures late (12-16 weeks) causing severe hemorrhage due to rich blood supply.

clinical.q: The classic triad of ectopic pregnancy includes all EXCEPT:
clinical.explain: Classic triad: amenorrhea, vaginal bleeding, and abdominal pain. Symptoms also include syncope and shoulder tip pain (Kehr's sign from blood irritating the diaphragm).

options: Ruptured ectopic pregnancy with intra-abdominal bleeding
clinical.explain: Cullen's sign — periumbilical blue discoloration — indicates intra-abdominal hemorrhage, which can occur in ruptured ectopic pregnancy.

investigations.q: The diagnostic test of choice for ectopic pregnancy is:
investigations.explain: Transvaginal ultrasound is the diagnostic test of choice. Absence of intrauterine gestational sac with positive hCG is suspicious for ectopic.

discriminatory.q: The discriminatory zone for hCG in diagnosing ectopic pregnancy is approximately:
discriminatory.explain: When hCG is above the discriminatory zone (1500-2000 IU/L) and no intrauterine gestational sac is seen, ectopic pregnancy is highly suspected.

treatment.q: Which of the following is a criterion for medical management of ectopic pregnancy with methotrexate?
options: Unruptured ectopic mass < 3.5 cm with low hCG
treatment.explain: Methotrexate is indicated for hemodynamically stable patients with unruptured ectopic, mass < 3.5 cm, and no fetal cardiac activity.

surgical.q: In a ruptured ectopic pregnancy with hemodynamic instability, the treatment of choice is:
surgical.explain: Ruptured ectopic with instability requires emergency laparotomy and salpingectomy. Salpingostomy (incision, remove, preserve tube) is for unruptured cases desiring fertility.

interstitial.q: Interstitial ectopic pregnancy is treated by:
interstitial.explain: Interstitial (cornual) ectopic pregnancy is treated by cornuostomy or cornual resection due to its location in the uterine cornua.

ovarian.q: Ovarian ectopic pregnancy is treated by:
ovarian.explain: Ovarian ectopic is managed by ovarian wedge resection or oophorectomy.
